subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player Components
- **Quilt boards** — 1 per player; hexagonal grid with spaces for tiles.
- **Goal tiles** — 6 different design goal tiles; each player places 3 on their board.
- **Board layout** — Each board has 22 placeable hex spaces surrounding 3 goal tile positions.

#### Tiles
- **Patch tiles** — 108 hexagonal fabric tiles.
- **6 colors** — Blue, yellow, green, purple, pink, and dark blue.
- **6 patterns** — Stripes, dots, fern, quatrefoil, flowers, and crosses.
- **Combination** — Each tile has one color and one pattern.

#### Scoring Components
- **Cat tokens** — 3 cat types used per game (from 6 available), each with specific pattern requirements.
- **Button tokens** — Score for groups of 3+ same-color tiles.
- **Design goal tiles** — Score for achieving specific patterns or color arrangements around goal positions.
- **Scoring reference** — Charts showing cat requirements and button values.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Distribute boards** — Give each player 1 quilt board.
2. **Select cats** — Choose 3 of the 6 available cat scoring tiles; place them where all can see.
3. **Select goal tiles** — Randomly deal goal tiles; each player places 3 goal tiles on their board's designated spots.
4. **Prepare tile supply** — Place all 108 patch tiles in the cloth bag.
5. **Form market** — Draw 3 tiles from the bag and place them face up as the shared market.
6. **Button supply** — Sort button tokens by color near the play area.
7. **Cat token supply** — Place cat tokens near their scoring cards.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Begins With
- **1 quilt board** — With 3 goal tiles pre-placed.
- **22 empty spaces** — All hex spaces are empty.
- **No tiles in hand** — Tiles are drafted from the market.

#### Goal Tile Placement
- **3 goal spots** — Each board has 3 designated positions for goal tiles.
- **Goal requirements** — Each goal tile specifies the pattern and/or color arrangement needed in the 6 surrounding hex spaces.
- **Scoring tiers** — Goals have a high-score and low-score threshold.

#### Starting Player
- **Random selection** — Choose a starting player randomly.
- **Clockwise play** — Turns proceed clockwise.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Market ready** — Ensure 3 tiles are face up in the market.
2. **First player drafts** — Choose 1 tile from the market.
3. **Place on board** — Put the drafted tile on any empty hex space.
4. **Refill market** — Draw 1 tile from bag to replace the taken tile.
5. **Check scoring** — Check if any buttons or cats are earned.
6. **Next player** — Pass to the next player clockwise.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Draft a tile** — Choose 1 of the 3 face-up tiles from the market.
2. **Place the tile** — Put it on any empty hex space on your quilt board.
3. **Check for buttons** — If you have created a group of 3 or more adjacent tiles of the same color, claim a button token of that color (if available).
4. **Check for cats** — If tiles adjacent to each other meet a cat's pattern requirement, place that cat token on your board.
5. **Refill market** — Draw 1 tile from the bag to refill the market to 3.
6. **Pass turn** — Next player clockwise.

#### Game Length
- **22 turns per player** — Each player places exactly 22 tiles to fill their board.
- **Game end** — The game ends when all players have filled their boards.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Drafting
- **Market of 3** — Always 3 tiles face up.
- **Choose any 1** — Take any of the 3 available tiles.
- **No holding** — You must place the tile immediately.

#### Placement
- **Any empty space** — Place on any unoccupied hex on your board.
- **No adjacency requirement** — Tiles do not need to be placed next to existing tiles.
- **Permanent** — Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.

#### Buttons (Color Scoring)
- **Group of 3+** — Three or more adjacent tiles of the same color earn a button.
- **One button per color** — You can only earn 1 button token per color (6 colors = max 6 buttons).
- **Button value** — Each button is worth 3 points.
- **Rainbow button** — If you earn all 6 color buttons, you receive a rainbow button worth bonus points.

#### Cats (Pattern Scoring)
- **Pattern requirements** — Each cat requires a specific combination of patterns on adjacent tiles.
- **Cat placement** — Place the cat token on your board when the requirement is met.
- **Cat values** — Different cats are worth different point values.
- **Multiple cats** — You can attract multiple cats of the same type if you create multiple qualifying groups.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Design Goals
- **3 goal tiles per board** — Placed during setup in the center of specific hex clusters.
- **Surrounding tiles** — The 6 hexes surrounding each goal tile must meet the goal's requirements.
- **Goal scoring tiers** — Each goal has two scoring levels:
  - **High score** — Fully meeting the pattern and color requirements.
  - **Low score** — Partially meeting the requirements.
  - **No score** — Failing to meet either threshold.
- **Goal variety** — Different goals require different combinations (e.g., all 6 different colors, 3 pairs of patterns, AAA-BBB color split).

#### Cat Scoring Details
- **6 available cats** — Millie, Tibbit, Coconut, Cira, Gwenivre, Leo (names vary).
- **3 cats per game** — Selected during setup for variety.
- **Pattern requirements** — Each cat needs specific pattern combinations on a group of adjacent tiles.
- **Example** — A cat might require 3 adjacent tiles with 3 different patterns, or a pair of tiles with matching patterns.

#### Color-Pattern Dual System
- **Every tile matters twice** — Each tile contributes to both color groups (buttons) and pattern groups (cats).
- **Tension** — Placing for color groups may conflict with pattern requirements and vice versa.
- **Goals add a third layer** — Goal tiles demand specific arrangements, further constraining choices.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **All boards full** — Game ends when every player has placed 22 tiles.

#### Final Scoring
- **Design goals** — Score each of your 3 goal tiles (0, low, or high value each).
- **Buttons** — 3 points per button token earned. Bonus for rainbow button (all 6 colors).
- **Cats** — Points per cat token attracted to your quilt.

#### Winner
- **Most points wins** — Sum design goals + buttons + cats.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with more buttons wins; if still tied, shared victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Study your goals first** — Understand what each of your 3 design goals requires before placing any tile.
- **Map out goal surroundings** — Plan which colors and patterns you need in the 6 spaces around each goal.
- **Start near goals** — Place early tiles around goal positions to lock in requirements.
- **Identify compatible cats** — See which cat requirements overlap with your goal needs.
- **Draft flexibly** — Early tiles should serve multiple scoring paths when possible.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring goals until late** — Goal tiles are surrounded by 6 spaces that fill up fast; delaying goal planning leads to impossible requirements.
- **Chasing buttons over goals** — Buttons are worth 3 each, but goals can be worth 10+; prioritize goals.
- **Forgetting cat requirements** — Cats require specific pattern combinations; placing without checking wastes opportunities.
- **Poor color distribution** — Clustering one color for buttons is good, but spreading others randomly prevents future groups.
- **Not watching the market** — Failing to plan around available tiles leads to forced suboptimal placements.
- **Overcommitting to one scoring type** — Balanced scoring across goals, buttons, and cats outperforms focusing on just one.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Color vs pattern priority** — When a tile serves button scoring but not cat scoring, decide which matters more for your board state.
- **Goal tile order** — Determine which goal to complete first based on available tiles in the market.
- **Defensive drafting** — Whether to take a tile that helps an opponent less, even if it is not your best pick.
- **Accept partial goals** — When a high-score goal becomes impossible, pivot to securing the low-score tier.
- **Button timing** — Whether to lock in a 3-tile color group now or extend it for future cat opportunities.
